text: Science has a clearer story about antibiotic spectrum than social media does. Antibiotic spectrum refers to the range of bacteria an antibiotic can affect. Broad-spectrum antibiotics target many bacteria, but narrow-spectrum ones zero in on specific types. Why does it matter? Overusing broad-spectrum antibiotics can lead to resistance. This could make future infections harder to treat. Understanding antibiotic spectrum helps us use them wisely. It’s about preserving their power. Stay informed and share this knowledge! Follow for more science insights.
